mitre_attack: T1590.001 - Gather Victim Network Information: Domain Properties
cwe: CWE-297 - Improper Validation of Certificate with Host Mismatch
CWE-296 - Improper Following of Chain of Trust for Certificate Validation
CWE-298 - Improper Validation of Certificate Expiration

threat.title: Certificate Validation Bypass Leading to Man-in-the-Middle Attacks
threat.description: When applications disable SSL/TLS certificate validation, accept self-signed certificates, skip hostname verification, or use weak cipher suites, attackers can perform man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks to intercept, decrypt, and modify encrypted communications. This vulnerability is particularly critical in mobile applications, IoT devices, and microservices architectures where mTLS is used for service-to-service authentication.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker positions themselves in network path between client and server (public WiFi, compromised router, ISP-level attack)
2. Client initiates HTTPS connection to legitimate server (e.g., api.bank.com)
3. Attacker intercepts connection and presents fraudulent certificate (self-signed or from untrusted CA)
4. Vulnerable application accepts invalid certificate due to disabled validation
5. Attacker decrypts all traffic, captures credentials, session tokens, and sensitive data
6. Attacker optionally modifies requests/responses (e.g., changing wire transfer amounts)
7. Attacker forwards modified traffic to legitimate server, establishing two separate TLS sessions
8. Both client and server remain unaware of the interception
prerequisites: Application disables certificate validation (rejectUnauthorized: false, SSL_VERIFY_NONE)
Application accepts self-signed certificates without pinning
Attacker has network-level access (ARP spoofing, DNS hijacking, rogue WiFi AP)
Target application uses vulnerable TLS implementation or configuration

cve_references.description: Heartbleed - OpenSSL buffer over-read vulnerability allowing private key extraction from server memory
cve_references.impact: Attackers could extract private keys, session cookies, and passwords from vulnerable servers

cve_references.description: FREAK (Factoring RSA Export Keys) - Forces downgrade to weak 512-bit export-grade RSA keys
cve_references.impact: MITM attackers could force weak encryption and decrypt TLS traffic

cve_references.description: POODLE (Padding Oracle On Downgraded Legacy Encryption) - SSL 3.0 CBC cipher vulnerability
cve_references.impact: Attackers could decrypt secure connections by exploiting CBC padding oracle

cve_references.description: OpenSSL padding oracle vulnerability in AES-NI CBC MAC check
cve_references.impact: MITM attackers could decrypt TLS traffic using timing attacks

cve_references.description: OpenSSL TLS 1.2 renegotiation denial of service
cve_references.impact: Attackers could trigger crashes in TLS 1.2 renegotiation

real_world_breaches.impact: Fraudulent certificates issued for *.google.com and 500+ other domains; used for mass surveillance in Iran; DigiNotar declared bankruptcy; entire CA infrastructure had to be distrusted globally
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Compromise of CA infrastructure allowed attackers to issue fraudulent certificates that were trusted by all browsers

real_world_breaches.impact: 9 fraudulent SSL certificates issued for high-profile domains (mail.google.com, login.yahoo.com, addons.mozilla.org)
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Registration authority compromise through SQL injection; certificates could enable MITM attacks
real_world_breaches.affected_users: Potentially millions of users of major web services

real_world_breaches.impact: Lenovo shipped laptops with Superfish adware that installed root CA certificate with known private key, enabling MITM attacks on all HTTPS traffic
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Pre-installed adware with hardcoded CA certificate private key ('komodia'); same key used across all installations
real_world_breaches.affected_users: Hundreds of thousands of Lenovo laptop owners

real_world_breaches.impact: Dell shipped computers with self-signed root certificate and private key, allowing decryption of all TLS traffic
real_world_breaches.root_cause: eDellRoot certificate installed with exportable private key for 'customer support purposes'
real_world_breaches.affected_users: Unknown number of Dell computer purchasers

real_world_breaches.incident: Mobile Banking Apps Certificate Validation Failures
real_world_breaches.impact: Research found 50+ mobile banking apps with disabled certificate validation, exposing millions of users to MITM attacks
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Developers disabled certificate validation during testing and forgot to re-enable for production
real_world_breaches.affected_users: Millions of mobile banking app users globally

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Certificate validation disabled in Android app allowing MITM attack to capture credentials and session tokens
iOS app accepts self-signed certificates without certificate pinning, enabling traffic interception
Microservices mTLS implementation accepts any certificate, bypassing service authentication

mitigations.title: Implement Mutual TLS (mTLS) with Proper Certificate Validation in Express.js
mitigations.description: Configure Express.js with strict certificate validation, hostname verification, and mutual TLS authentication for API endpoints. This ensures both client and server authenticate each other using certificates.
iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Generate CA certificate and server/client certificate pairs using OpenSSL
Verify rejectUnauthorized is set to true in all HTTPS configurations
Test with invalid certificate (should reject connection)
Test with expired certificate (should reject)
Test with wrong hostname (should reject)
Test with self-signed certificate not in CA chain (should reject)
Use tools like ssllabs.com or testssl.sh to verify TLS configuration
Monitor TLS handshake failures in logs

mitigations.title: Implement Certificate Pinning in Spring Boot Microservices
mitigations.description: Use certificate pinning to validate that the server certificate matches a known, trusted certificate or public key. This prevents MITM attacks even if a rogue CA issues fraudulent certificates.
iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
validation_steps: Generate server certificate and extract SHA-256 fingerprint: openssl x509 -in cert.pem -pubkey -noout | openssl pkey -pubin -outform der | openssl dgst -sha256 -binary | openssl enc -base64
Add fingerprint to PINNED_CERTIFICATES set
Test connection to valid server (should succeed)
Test with proxy presenting different certificate (should fail with CertificateException)
Update pins before certificate rotation (support multiple pins for rotation)
Monitor logs for certificate pinning failures
Set up alerts for repeated pinning failures (potential attack)

mitigations.title: Secure Certificate Validation in Python Requests Library
mitigations.description: Configure Python requests library with strict certificate validation, custom CA bundles, and certificate pinning using urllib3.

code_example.code:
import requests
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
from urllib3.poolmanager import PoolManager
from urllib3.util.ssl_ import create_urllib3_context
import ssl
import certifi
import hashlib
from typing import Set
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Certificate pinning configuration
PINNED_CERT_FINGERPRINTS: Set[str] = {
    'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855',
    '2cf42702a78fd95c3e9d1c7a6b4e8f2d3a1b9c4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e'
}

class SSLPinningAdapter(HTTPAdapter):
    """Custom HTTPS adapter with certificate pinning."""
    
    def __init__(self, pinned_fingerprints: Set[str], *args, **kwargs):
        self.pinned_fingerprints = pinned_fingerprints
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    
    def init_poolmanager(self, *args, **kwargs):
        """Initialize pool manager with custom SSL context."""
        context = create_urllib3_context()
        
        # Use only TLS 1.2 and 1.3
        context.minimum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_2
        context.maximum_version = ssl.TLSVersion.TLSv1_3
        
        # Strong cipher suites only
        context.set_ciphers(
            'TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:'
            'TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:'
            'TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256:'
            'ECDHE+AESGCM:'
            'ECDHE+CHACHA20'
        )
        
        # Disable compression (CRIME attack mitigation)
        context.options |= ssl.OP_NO_COMPRESSION
        
        # Load system CA certificates
        context.load_verify_locations(certifi.where())
        
        # Enable hostname verification
        context.check_hostname = True
        context.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_REQUIRED
        
        kwargs['ssl_context'] = context
        return super().init_poolmanager(*args, **kwargs)
    
    def cert_verify(self, conn, url, verify, cert):
        """Verify certificate pinning after standard validation."""
        # First, perform standard certificate validation
        super().cert_verify(conn, url, verify, cert)
        
        # Then check certificate pinning
        sock = conn.sock
        if sock:
            # Get peer certificate
            cert_der = sock.getpeercert(binary_form=True)
            if cert_der:
                # Compute SHA-256 fingerprint
                fingerprint = hashlib.sha256(cert_der).hexdigest()
                
                # Check against pinned certificates
                if fingerprint not in self.pinned_fingerprints:
                    raise ssl.SSLError(
                        f"Certificate pinning failed. "
                        f"Server certificate fingerprint {fingerprint} "
                        f"does not match any pinned certificates. "
                        f"Possible MITM attack detected."
                    )
                
                logger.info(f"Certificate pinning validated for {url}")

# Example 1: Basic secure requests with certificate validation
def secure_get_request(url: str) -> requests.Response:
    """Make HTTPS request with strict certificate validation."""
    try:
        response = requests.get(
            url,
            verify=True,  # CRITICAL: Never set to False
            timeout=5,
            headers={'User-Agent': 'SecureApp/1.0'}
        )
        response.raise_for_status()
        return response
    except requests.exceptions.SSLError as e:
        logger.error(f"SSL certificate validation failed for {url}: {e}")
        raise
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        logger.error(f"Request failed for {url}: {e}")
        raise

# Example 2: Request with certificate pinning
def secure_get_with_pinning(url: str) -> requests.Response:
    """Make HTTPS request with certificate pinning."""
    session = requests.Session()
    session.mount('https://', SSLPinningAdapter(PINNED_CERT_FINGERPRINTS))
    
    try:
        response = session.get(
            url,
            verify=True,
            timeout=5
        )
        response.raise_for_status()
        return response
    except ssl.SSLError as e:
        logger.error(f"Certificate pinning validation failed: {e}")
        # Alert security team - potential MITM attack
        raise
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        logger.error(f"Request failed: {e}")
        raise
    finally:
        session.close()

# Example 3: Mutual TLS (mTLS) with client certificate
def mtls_request(url: str, client_cert_path: str, client_key_path: str) -> requests.Response:
    """Make HTTPS request with mutual TLS authentication."""
    session = requests.Session()
    session.mount('https://', SSLPinningAdapter(PINNED_CERT_FINGERPRINTS))
    
    try:
        response = session.get(
            url,
            cert=(client_cert_path, client_key_path),  # Client certificate and key
            verify=True,  # Verify server certificate
            timeout=5
        )
        response.raise_for_status()
        return response
    except requests.exceptions.SSLError as e:
        logger.error(f"mTLS authentication failed: {e}")
        raise
    finally:
        session.close()

# Example 4: Using custom CA bundle
def request_with_custom_ca(url: str, ca_bundle_path: str) -> requests.Response:
    """Make HTTPS request with custom CA certificate bundle."""
    try:
        response = requests.get(
            url,
            verify=ca_bundle_path,  # Path to custom CA bundle
            timeout=5
        )
        response.raise_for_status()
        return response
    except requests.exceptions.SSLError as e:
        logger.error(f"Certificate validation failed with custom CA: {e}")
        raise

# Utility: Extract certificate fingerprint for pinning
def get_certificate_fingerprint(hostname: str, port: int = 443) -> str:
    """Get SHA-256 fingerprint of server certificate (for initial setup)."""
    import socket
    
    context = ssl.create_default_context()
    with socket.create_connection((hostname, port)) as sock:
        with context.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname=hostname) as ssock:
            cert_der = ssock.getpeercert(binary_form=True)
            fingerprint = hashlib.sha256(cert_der).hexdigest()
            
            # Also get certificate details
            cert = ssock.getpeercert()
            logger.info(f"Certificate for {hostname}:")
            logger.info(f"  Subject: {dict(x[0] for x in cert['subject'])}")
            logger.info(f"  Issuer: {dict(x[0] for x in cert['issuer'])}")
            logger.info(f"  Valid from: {cert['notBefore']}")
            logger.info(f"  Valid until: {cert['notAfter']}")
            logger.info(f"  SHA-256 Fingerprint: {fingerprint}")
            
            return fingerprint

# Example usage
if __name__ == '__main__':
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    
    # Example 1: Basic secure request
    try:
        response = secure_get_request('https://api.example.com/data')
        print(f"Response: {response.status_code}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Request failed: {e}")
    
    # Example 2: Get certificate fingerprint (for pinning setup)
    try:
        fingerprint = get_certificate_fingerprint('api.example.com')
        print(f"Add this to PINNED_CERT_FINGERPRINTS: {fingerprint}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Failed to get fingerprint: {e}")
    
    # Example 3: Request with certificate pinning
    try:
        response = secure_get_with_pinning('https://api.example.com/secure')
        print(f"Pinned request successful: {response.status_code}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Pinned request failed: {e}")
    
    # Example 4: Mutual TLS request
    try:
        response = mtls_request(
            'https://api.example.com/mtls',
            '/path/to/client-cert.pem',
            '/path/to/client-key.pem'
        )
        print(f"mTLS request successful: {response.status_code}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"mTLS request failed: {e}")

# CRITICAL: Common mistakes to avoid
# ❌ NEVER DO THIS:
# requests.get(url, verify=False)  # Disables all certificate validation
# 
# ❌ NEVER DO THIS:
# import urllib3
# urllib3.disable_warnings(urllib3.exceptions.InsecureRequestWarning)
# 
# ❌ NEVER DO THIS:
# os.environ['PYTHONHTTPSVERIFY'] = '0'
# 
# ✅ ALWAYS DO THIS:
# requests.get(url, verify=True)  # or verify='/path/to/ca-bundle.pem'

iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
validation_steps: Install certifi for up-to-date CA bundle: pip install certifi
Verify requests library uses certifi by default
Extract certificate fingerprint using get_certificate_fingerprint()
Add fingerprint to PINNED_CERT_FINGERPRINTS set
Test with valid certificate (should succeed)
Test with mitmproxy or Burp Suite (should fail with SSL error)
Monitor application logs for SSL errors
Set up alerting for certificate pinning failures

mitigations.title: Implement Certificate Lifecycle Management and Rotation
mitigations.description: Automate certificate issuance, renewal, and rotation using cert-manager in Kubernetes or Azure Key Vault certificate management to prevent expired certificates and enable zero-downtime rotation.

code_example.code:
# Install cert-manager in Kubernetes cluster
# kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.13.0/cert-manager.yaml

---
# ClusterIssuer for Let's Encrypt (production)
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: ClusterIssuer
metadata:
  name: letsencrypt-prod
spec:
  acme:
    # Let's Encrypt production server
    server: https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
    email: security@example.com
    privateKeySecretRef:
      name: letsencrypt-prod-account-key
    # HTTP-01 challenge for domain validation
    solvers:
    - http01:
        ingress:
          class: nginx

---
# Certificate resource for automatic issuance and renewal
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
metadata:
  name: api-tls-certificate
  namespace: production
spec:
  # Certificate subject
  commonName: api.example.com
  dnsNames:
  - api.example.com
  - api-internal.example.com
  
  # Certificate issuer
  issuerRef:
    name: letsencrypt-prod
    kind: ClusterIssuer
  
  # Secret to store certificate and private key
  secretName: api-tls-secret
  
  # Certificate validity period (Let's Encrypt issues 90-day certs)
  duration: 2160h  # 90 days
  renewBefore: 720h  # Renew 30 days before expiration
  
  # Key algorithm and size
  privateKey:
    algorithm: RSA
    encoding: PKCS1
    size: 2048
  
  # Certificate usages
  usages:
  - digital signature
  - key encipherment
  - server auth
  
  # Subject Alternative Names (SANs)
  subject:
    organizations:
    - Example Corporation
    countries:
    - US
    organizationalUnits:
    - Engineering
    localities:
    - San Francisco
    provinces:
    - California

---
# Certificate for mutual TLS (client authentication)
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
metadata:
  name: mtls-client-certificate
  namespace: production
spec:
  commonName: service-a.internal
  dnsNames:
  - service-a.internal
  - service-a.production.svc.cluster.local
  
  issuerRef:
    name: internal-ca
    kind: ClusterIssuer
  
  secretName: mtls-client-secret
  
  duration: 8760h  # 1 year for internal certs
  renewBefore: 2160h  # Renew 90 days before expiration
  
  privateKey:
    algorithm: RSA
    size: 2048
  
  usages:
  - digital signature
  - key encipherment
  - client auth  # Client authentication usage

---
# Internal CA issuer for microservices mTLS
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: ClusterIssuer
metadata:
  name: internal-ca
spec:
  ca:
    secretName: internal-ca-key-pair

---
# Ingress with automatic TLS
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: api-ingress
  namespace: production
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-protocols: "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-ciphers: "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-prefer-server-ciphers: "true"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/force-ssl-redirect: "true"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - api.example.com
    secretName: api-tls-secret  # Automatically managed by cert-manager
  rules:
  - host: api.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: api-service
            port:
              number: 443

---
# CertificateRequest for monitoring
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: certificate-monitoring
  namespace: production
data:
  check-certificates.sh: |
    #!/bin/bash
    # Monitor certificate expiration
    kubectl get certificates -A -o json | jq -r '
      .items[] | 
      select(.status.notAfter != null) |
      "\(.metadata.namespace)/\(.metadata.name): expires \(.status.notAfter)"
    '
    
    # Alert on certificates expiring within 30 days
    kubectl get certificates -A -o json | jq -r '
      .items[] |
      select(.status.notAfter != null) |
      select(
        (now | todateiso8601) as $now |
        (.status.notAfter | fromdateiso8601) as $expiry |
        ($expiry - ($now | fromdateiso8601)) < (30 * 24 * 60 * 60)
      ) |
      "WARNING: \(.metadata.namespace)/\(.metadata.name) expires soon: \(.status.notAfter)"
    '

---
# Azure Key Vault integration (alternative to cert-manager)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: azure-keyvault-config
data:
  keyvault.yaml: |
    # Azure Key Vault certificate configuration
    vault_url: https://my-vault.vault.azure.net
    certificates:
      - name: api-certificate
        dns_names:
          - api.example.com
        auto_renew: true
        renew_before_days: 30
        key_size: 2048
        key_type: RSA
        validity_months: 12
        issuer: DigiCert  # or Let's Encrypt, GlobalSign, etc.
    
    # Certificate rotation policy
    rotation_policy:
      auto_rotate: true
      rotate_before_expiry_days: 30
      notification_email: security@example.com
      notification_days_before: [60, 30, 7, 1]

---
# Prometheus ServiceMonitor for cert-manager metrics
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: cert-manager
  namespace: cert-manager
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: cert-manager
  endpoints:
  - port: tcp-prometheus-servicemonitor
    interval: 60s
    path: /metrics

# Example Prometheus alerts for certificate expiration
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: prometheus-certificate-alerts
data:
  alerts.yml: |
    groups:
    - name: certificate-expiration
      interval: 1h
      rules:
      - alert: CertificateExpiringIn7Days
        expr: certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds - time() < 7 * 24 * 60 * 60
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "Certificate {{ $labels.name }} expires in less than 7 days"
          description: "Certificate {{ $labels.name }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} expires on {{ $value | humanizeTimestamp }}"
      
      - alert: CertificateExpiringIn1Day
        expr: certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds - time() < 24 * 60 * 60
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Certificate {{ $labels.name }} expires in less than 1 day!"
          description: "URGENT: Certificate {{ $labels.name }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} expires on {{ $value | humanizeTimestamp }}"
      
      - alert: CertificateRenewalFailed
        expr: certmanager_certificate_ready_status{condition="False"} == 1
        for: 1h
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Certificate {{ $labels.name }} renewal failed"
          description: "Certificate {{ $labels.name }} in namespace {{ $labels.namespace }} failed to renew. Check cert-manager logs."

nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
validation_steps: Install cert-manager in Kubernetes cluster
Create ClusterIssuer for Let's Encrypt or internal CA
Deploy Certificate resources with appropriate renewal windows
Verify automatic certificate issuance: kubectl describe certificate
Test automatic renewal by setting short validity period
Set up Prometheus monitoring for certificate expiration
Configure alerts for certificates expiring within 30 days
Test certificate rotation with zero downtime

indicators: Unexpected TLS certificate errors in application logs
Repeated TLS handshake failures from same client IP
TLS connections using deprecated protocol versions (SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1)
Weak cipher suites in use (RC4, DES, 3DES, export ciphers)
Certificate expiration dates in the past or far future
Certificate Common Name (CN) mismatches with hostname
OCSP responder failures or revoked certificate warnings
Unusual number of certificate-related exceptions in application logs

log_queries.query:
// Detect TLS certificate validation failures
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeBetween >= ago(1h)
| where ScStatus in (495, 496, 497)  // Nginx SSL error codes
| summarize FailureCount = count() by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where FailureCount > 10
| order by FailureCount desc

// Detect connections with weak TLS versions
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where Protocol in ("SSL/3.0", "TLS/1.0", "TLS/1.1")
| summarize ConnectionCount = count() by ClientIP, Protocol
| order by ConnectionCount desc

// Detect certificate expiration approaching
AzureDiagnostics
| where ResourceType == "VAULTS"
| where OperationName == "CertificateNearExpiry"
| where DaysUntilExpiry_d < 30
| project TimeGenerated, CertificateName=Resource, DaysUntilExpiry_d, SubscriptionId
| order by DaysUntilExpiry_d asc

# TLS handshake failures indicating potential MITM
index=application_logs sourcetype=nginx:error
| search "SSL_do_handshake() failed" OR "certificate verify failed" OR "SSL: error"
| stats count by client_ip, error_message, _time
| where count > 5
| table _time, client_ip, error_message, count
| sort -count

# Detect deprecated TLS protocol usage
index=network_logs sourcetype=tls_handshake
| search tls_version IN ("SSLv3", "TLSv1.0", "TLSv1.1")
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, tls_version, cipher_suite
| where count > 1
| table src_ip, dest_ip, tls_version, cipher_suite, count

# Certificate validation disabled in application code
index=application_logs sourcetype=python:app OR sourcetype=nodejs:app
| search "verify=False" OR "rejectUnauthorized: false" OR "SSL_VERIFY_NONE" OR "CERT_NONE"
| stats count by host, source, _time
| table _time, host, source, count

# TLS certificate validation errors
event.category: "network" AND event.outcome: "failure" AND tls.established: false
AND (error.message: (*certificate* OR *SSL* OR *TLS*) )

# Weak cipher suite usage
tls.cipher: ("TLS_RSA_WITH_RC4*" OR "*_3DES_*" OR "*_DES_*" OR "*_EXPORT_*")

# Self-signed certificate acceptance
tls.server.issuer: tls.server.subject

# Certificate expiration monitoring
tls.server.not_after: <now+30d AND tls.server.not_after: >now

# Application Load Balancer TLS errors
fields @timestamp, @message, client_ip, target_status_code
| filter target_status_code in [495, 496, 497]
| stats count() by client_ip, target_status_code
| filter count > 10
| sort count desc

# CloudTrail - Certificate Manager events
fields eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.principalId, requestParameters
| filter eventSource = "acm.amazonaws.com"
| filter eventName in ["ImportCertificate", "DeleteCertificate", "UpdateCertificateOptions"]
| sort eventTime desc

# Lambda function SSL/TLS errors
fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /SSLError|certificate verify failed|CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED/
| stats count() by bin(5m)
| sort @timestamp desc

tools.name: mitmproxy
tools.type: Interactive HTTPS proxy for testing certificate validation

tools.name: Burp Suite
tools.type: Web security testing with SSL interception
tools.usage: Configure as proxy to test certificate pinning bypass

manual_verification: Use mitmproxy or Burp Suite to intercept HTTPS traffic and present invalid certificate
Verify application rejects connection and logs certificate validation error
Test with expired certificate using openssl s_server
